title: sabularia uniflora
intro: Sabularia uniflora, also known as the blue sage or rosin root, is a perennial plant native to North America that has been used for centuries in herbal medicine. The plant's roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of sabularia uniflora include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Sabularia uniflora is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to promote skin health and wound healing.
